1.Bryce TeodosioRHBLAA vs Blake Snell (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.122 (-193 pts)K 44.2%BB 2.7%HR 1.3%Hit 9.4%Strong Pitcher

Bryce Teodosio RHB vs Blake Snell LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 164+522+43, pitcher rows 77+1570+1774

Verdict

Snell's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Teodosio should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.122 xwOBA (-193 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.131 -> final 0.122); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Pitch-mix projection

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
4-Seam Fastball43.00.1950.311GB 39% / LD 25% / FB 28% / PU 8%0.196+10.1920.1640.22427.0
Changeup27.40.0960.246GB 65% / LD 21% / FB 14% / PU 0%0.080-150.0120.0600.00066.9
Curveball24.20.2100.211GB 45% / LD 32% / FB 17% / PU 6%0.216+60.1110.0540.00063.3
Slider5.30.3920.283GB 50% / LD 25% / FB 21% / PU 5%0.383-90.3510.2790.41833.4
Sinker0.00.4190.262GB 50% / LD 25% / FB 21% / PU 5%0.412-80.3590.3400.48211.9

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Career = batter's full platoon-filtered BBE distribution. Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

Coverage % = share of the pitcher's attack on this pitch landing in zones with batter data; remainder imputed at league baselines (xwOBA 0.315, xBA 0.245, xSLG 0.405) so two batters with different coverage maps stay comparable.

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 65.8 mph
swing length 6.7 ft
attack angle 8.3°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+2.229-2.9on plane
Changeup-7.7+19.810+12.1swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.7+8.55-2.1on plane
Slider-8.3+10.65+2.3on plane

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

2.Petey HalpinLHBCLE vs Andrew Abbott (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.138 (-177 pts)K 19.5%BB 0.0%HR 1.7%Hit 12.2%Strong Pitcher

Petey Halpin LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 89+33+0, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.138 xwOBA (-177 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -12 pts (raw 0.150 -> adj 0.138); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

3.Joey WiemerRHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.138 (-177 pts)K 43.1%BB 6.5%HR 0.4%Hit 10.0%Strong Pitcher

Joey Wiemer RHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 293+223+109, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Joey Wiemer should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.138 xwOBA (-177 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.154 -> final 0.138); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 66.9 mph
swing length 7.8 ft
attack angle 6.7°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.0-0.418-5.4swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.7+15.67+7.9swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.7+6.7*0-4.0swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-8.3+14.89+6.5swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 5.Zach McKinstryLHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.168 (-147 pts)K 28.3%BB 7.6%HR 1.1%Hit 10.0%Strong Pitcher

Zach McKinstry LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 296+2180+1278, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Zach McKinstry should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.168 xwOBA (-147 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.175 -> adj 0.158); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.158 -> final 0.168); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

6.Hayden SengerRHBNYM vs Cam Schlittler (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.178 (-137 pts)K 24.4%BB 2.9%HR 0.0%Hit 17.8%Strong Pitcher

Hayden Senger RHB vs Cam Schlittler RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 11+254+0, pitcher rows 792+1448+0

Verdict

Schlittler's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Hayden Senger should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.178 xwOBA (-137 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.211 -> adj 0.201); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -22 pts (bb_type-adj 0.201 -> final 0.178); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

7.Drew RomoLHBCWS vs Edward Cabrera (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.190 (-125 pts)K 26.9%BB 15.9%HR 1.5%Hit 7.0%Strong Pitcher

Drew Romo LHB vs Edward Cabrera RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 151+12+189, pitcher rows 725+2253+1734

Verdict

Cabrera's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Romo should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.190 xwOBA (-125 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.302 -> adj 0.309); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -119 pts (bb_type-adj 0.309 -> final 0.190); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Drew Romo is on a 14-day slump (-58 pts vs season). Edward Cabrera is in a 14-day rough patch (+63 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

9.Ozzie AlbiesLHBATL vs Connelly Early (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.202 (-113 pts)K 15.9%BB 1.6%HR 1.4%Hit 17.3%Strong Pitcher

Ozzie Albies LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 672+2411+1686, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ozzie Albies should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.202 xwOBA (-113 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -40 pts (raw 0.235 -> adj 0.196); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.196 -> final 0.202); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ozzie Albies is on a 14-day slump (-43 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

10.Nasim NuñezLHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.203 (-112 pts)K 23.7%BB 11.7%HR 1.0%Hit 11.6%Strong Pitcher

Nasim Nuñez LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 597+418+327, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Nasim Nuñez should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.203 xwOBA (-112 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +8 pts (raw 0.207 -> adj 0.215); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.215 -> final 0.203); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

11.Mike YastrzemskiLHBATL vs Connelly Early (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.204 (-111 pts)K 40.0%BB 4.3%HR 1.7%Hit 10.4%Strong Pitcher

Mike Yastrzemski LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 532+2321+1960, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Mike Yastrzemski should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.204 xwOBA (-111 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.209 -> adj 0.192); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.192 -> final 0.204); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Mike Yastrzemski is on a 14-day heater (+41 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bat-tracking interaction

bat speed 72.8 mph
swing length 7.7 ft
attack angle 6.8°
PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-5.0+1.564-3.5swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Changeup-7.7+17.026+9.4swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk
Curveball-10.7+6.8*5-3.8swing flatter than pitch — topped / grounder risk
Slider-8.3+8.612+0.3on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. 13.Myles StrawRHBTOR vs Brenan Hanifee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.205 (-110 pts)K 9.8%BB 2.0%HR 0.2%Hit 18.5%Strong Pitcher

Myles Straw RHB vs Brenan Hanifee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 301+1178+14, pitcher rows 112+966+481

Verdict

Hanifee's sinker is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.205 xwOBA (-110 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -7 pts (bb_type-adj 0.212 -> final 0.205); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

14.Wenceel PérezLHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.209 (-106 pts)K 29.1%BB 6.5%HR 1.2%Hit 15.0%Strong Pitcher

Wenceel Pérez LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 337+1620+1829, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Wenceel Pérez should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.209 xwOBA (-106 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.194 -> final 0.209); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Wenceel Pérez is on a 14-day slump (-44 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

15.Billy CookRHBPIT vs Aaron Nola (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.210 (-105 pts)K 43.0%BB 0.0%HR 3.0%Hit 17.1%Strong Pitcher

Billy Cook RHB vs Aaron Nola RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 65+20+184, pitcher rows 734+1599+3280

Verdict

Nola's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage, while Billy Cook should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.210 xwOBA (-105 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.224 -> adj 0.210); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Aaron Nola is in a 14-day hot stretch (-60 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.216 xwOBA (-99 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +31 pts (bb_type-adj 0.185 -> final 0.216); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Daniel Schneemann is on a 14-day slump (-48 pts vs season). Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Chase DeLauter LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 615+27+0, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Chase DeLauter should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.218 xwOBA (-97 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -11 pts (raw 0.229 -> adj 0.218); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

18.Colt KeithLHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.220 (-95 pts)K 28.3%BB 7.4%HR 2.0%Hit 15.2%Edge Pitcher

Colt Keith LHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 464+1887+2147, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Colt Keith should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.220 xwOBA (-95 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.231 -> adj 0.210); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +10 pts (bb_type-adj 0.210 -> final 0.220); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Colt Keith is on a 14-day slump (-54 pts vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

19.Jorbit VivasLHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.221 (-94 pts)K 19.3%BB 5.5%HR 0.9%Hit 14.6%Edge Pitcher

Jorbit Vivas LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 329+246+0, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorbit Vivas should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.221 xwOBA (-94 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -13 pts (raw 0.217 -> adj 0.204); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +17 pts (bb_type-adj 0.204 -> final 0.221); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Verdict

Ryan's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.224 xwOBA (-91 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -14 pts (raw 0.233 -> adj 0.219); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +5 pts (bb_type-adj 0.219 -> final 0.224); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
4-Seam Fastball-3.8+2.149-1.7on plane

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

21.Jake RogersRHBDET vs Trey Yesavage (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.226 (-89 pts)K 38.8%BB 9.1%HR 2.1%Hit 13.6%Edge Pitcher

Jake Rogers RHB vs Trey Yesavage RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 168+574+1362, pitcher rows 243+683+0

Verdict

Yesavage's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Jake Rogers should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.226 xwOBA (-89 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -10 pts (raw 0.237 -> adj 0.227); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

22.Ben WilliamsonRHBTB vs Janson Junk (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.227 (-88 pts)K 18.0%BB 1.8%HR 0.0%Hit 22.9%Edge Pitcher

Ben Williamson RHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 507+1216+0, pitcher rows 677+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Ben Williamson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.227 xwOBA (-88 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.243 -> adj 0.234); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -8 pts (bb_type-adj 0.234 -> final 0.227); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Ben Williamson is on a 14-day heater (+74 pts vs season). Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Travis Bazzana LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 242+0+0, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's 4-seam fastball is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -9 pts (raw 0.238 -> adj 0.228); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Michael Harris II LHB vs Connelly Early LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 534+2181+1718, pitcher rows 712+401+0

Verdict

Early's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Michael Harris II should look to drive the curveball. Projection: 0.228 xwOBA (-87 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.216 -> final 0.228); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Michael Harris II is on a 14-day slump (-107 pts vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

25.Drew MillasLHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.230 (-85 pts)K 19.4%BB 6.5%HR 1.0%Hit 16.7%Edge Pitcher

Drew Millas LHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 281+209+252, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's knuckle curve is the projected matchup advantage, while Drew Millas should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.230 xwOBA (-85 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -24 pts (raw 0.249 -> adj 0.225); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

26.Brooks LeeRHBMIN vs Joe Ryan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.233 (-82 pts)K 22.9%BB 4.5%HR 2.5%Hit 18.3%Edge Pitcher

Brooks Lee RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 610+2010+621, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Brooks Lee should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.233 xwOBA (-82 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -6 pts (raw 0.238 -> adj 0.232); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

27.Justin CrawfordLHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.234 (-81 pts)K 26.6%BB 10.3%HR 0.8%Hit 14.4%Edge Pitcher

Justin Crawford LHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 491+0+0, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Justin Crawford should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.234 xwOBA (-81 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +6 pts (raw 0.243 -> adj 0.249); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -15 pts (bb_type-adj 0.249 -> final 0.234); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

28.Adolis GarcíaRHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.237 (-78 pts)K 24.1%BB 3.3%HR 2.8%Hit 18.6%Edge Pitcher

Adolis García RHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 708+2154+2542, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Adolis García should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.237 xwOBA (-78 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -21 pts (raw 0.238 -> adj 0.217); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.217 -> final 0.237); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Adolis García is on a 14-day slump (-42 pts vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Verdict

Mahle's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.241 xwOBA (-74 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -19 pts (raw 0.257 -> adj 0.239); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Jeff McNeil is on a 14-day slump (-67 pts vs season). Tyler Mahle is in a 14-day hot stretch (-75 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

31.Brady HouseRHBWSH vs Shane Baz (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.245 (-70 pts)K 27.4%BB 4.2%HR 0.3%Hit 22.5%Edge Pitcher

Brady House RHB vs Shane Baz RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 590+969+0, pitcher rows 774+2724+1207

Verdict

Baz's cutter is the projected matchup advantage, while Brady House should look to drive the slider. Projection: 0.245 xwOBA (-70 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.231 -> adj 0.216); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +29 pts (bb_type-adj 0.216 -> final 0.245); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Brady House is on a 14-day heater (+48 pts vs season). Shane Baz is in a 14-day rough patch (+53 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Austin Hedges RHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 239+709+598, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Hedges should look to drive the cutter. Projection: 0.246 xwOBA (-69 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +26 pts (raw 0.222 -> adj 0.247); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

34.Eric HaaseRHBSF vs Aaron Civale (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.247 (-68 pts)K 30.9%BB 2.4%HR 4.0%Hit 18.0%Edge Pitcher

Eric Haase RHB vs Aaron Civale RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 74+339+270, pitcher rows 719+1803+2735

Verdict

Civale's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Eric Haase should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.247 xwOBA (-68 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +7 pts (raw 0.282 -> adj 0.289); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -42 pts (bb_type-adj 0.289 -> final 0.247); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

35.Alec BohmRHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.249 (-66 pts)K 15.0%BB 3.7%HR 1.4%Hit 21.5%Edge Pitcher

Alec Bohm RHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 552+1867+2170, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Alec Bohm should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.249 xwOBA (-66 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -27 pts (raw 0.267 -> adj 0.240); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.240 -> final 0.249); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Luke Keaschall RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 687+775+0, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Luke Keaschall should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.251 xwOBA (-64 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.260 -> final 0.251); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

37.Oswald PerazaRHBLAA vs Blake Snell (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.253 (-62 pts)K 37.7%BB 6.6%HR 2.7%Hit 19.4%Edge Pitcher

Oswald Peraza RHB vs Blake Snell LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 436+1005+40, pitcher rows 77+1570+1774

Verdict

Snell's curveball is the projected matchup advantage, while Oswald Peraza should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.253 xwOBA (-62 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +15 pts (raw 0.218 -> adj 0.233); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +20 pts (bb_type-adj 0.233 -> final 0.253); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

38.Jorge SolerRHBLAA vs Blake Snell (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.253 (-62 pts)K 36.5%BB 13.2%HR 1.6%Hit 14.4%Edge Pitcher

Jorge Soler RHB vs Blake Snell LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 674+1291+2388, pitcher rows 77+1570+1774

Verdict

Snell's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Jorge Soler should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.253 xwOBA (-62 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -22 pts (raw 0.266 -> adj 0.244); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.244 -> final 0.253); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs LHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

39.Hunter FeducciaLHBTB vs Janson Junk (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.255 (-60 pts)K 19.0%BB 10.1%HR 0.0%Hit 18.4%Edge Pitcher

Hunter Feduccia LHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 227+477+57, pitcher rows 677+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's changeup is the projected matchup advantage, while Hunter Feduccia should look to drive the sweeper. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.297 -> adj 0.307); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -52 pts (bb_type-adj 0.307 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs RHP — opposite-side platoon advantage to the hitter.

40.Austin MartinRHBMIN vs Joe Ryan (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.255 (-60 pts)K 24.1%BB 6.7%HR 0.2%Hit 20.6%Edge Pitcher

Austin Martin RHB vs Joe Ryan RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 533+749+934, pitcher rows 660+2798+2108

Verdict

Ryan's split-finger is the projected matchup advantage, while Austin Martin should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.255 xwOBA (-60 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -29 pts (raw 0.293 -> adj 0.264); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -9 pts (bb_type-adj 0.264 -> final 0.255); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Joe Ryan is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Projected re-weights each pitch-specific BBE distribution by the pitcher's marginal pitch usage; pitches with <5 weighted BBE fall back to the batter's overall mix. Δ (pp) is the projected share minus career share, in percentage points. ★ rows mark the highlighted 10–26° barrel zone (overlaps Statcast's broader 8–32° sweet-spot range).

42.Kyle ManzardoLHBCLE vs Andrew Abbott (LHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.259 (-56 pts)K 27.7%BB 4.4%HR 5.3%Hit 18.7%Edge Pitcher

Kyle Manzardo LHB vs Andrew Abbott LHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 548+2074+709, pitcher rows 705+2677+2334

Verdict

Abbott's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Kyle Manzardo should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.259 xwOBA (-56 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -8 pts (raw 0.235 -> adj 0.226); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +32 pts (bb_type-adj 0.226 -> final 0.259); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Kyle Manzardo is on a 14-day heater (+116 pts vs season). Andrew Abbott is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). LHB vs LHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

Bibee's sinker is the projected matchup advantage, while Blake Dunn should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.262 xwOBA (-53 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -15 pts (raw 0.288 -> adj 0.273); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -12 pts (bb_type-adj 0.273 -> final 0.262); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tanner Bibee is in a 14-day hot stretch (-49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

* fewer than 5 swings on this pitch type; showing batter's overall attack angle as a fallback.

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Verdict

Scholtens' sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Javier Sanoja should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.262 xwOBA (-53 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +10 pts (raw 0.255 -> adj 0.265); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

45.Jonny DeLucaRHBTB vs Janson Junk (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.265 (-50 pts)K 16.6%BB 1.0%HR 0.8%Hit 25.4%Slight Pitcher

Jonny DeLuca RHB vs Janson Junk RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 351+225+1350, pitcher rows 677+1622+175

Verdict

Junk's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage. Projection: 0.265 xwOBA (-50 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: +25 pts (raw 0.234 -> adj 0.259); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward stronger contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +6 pts (bb_type-adj 0.259 -> final 0.265); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Janson Junk is in a 14-day rough patch (+41 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

47.Bryce JohnsonRHBSD vs Emerson Hancock (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.270 (-45 pts)K 19.4%BB 2.2%HR 0.3%Hit 21.2%Slight Pitcher

Bryce Johnson RHB vs Emerson Hancock RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 114+298+263, pitcher rows 741+1498+1015

Verdict

Hancock's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Bryce Johnson should look to drive the sinker. Projection: 0.270 xwOBA (-45 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -17 pts (raw 0.303 -> adj 0.286); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: -16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.286 -> final 0.270); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into worse counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Emerson Hancock is in a 14-day hot stretch (-44 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

48.Tyler StephensonRHBCIN vs Tanner Bibee (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.273 (-42 pts)K 30.4%BB 6.8%HR 2.4%Hit 20.0%Slight Pitcher

Tyler Stephenson RHB vs Tanner Bibee RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 466+1424+1956, pitcher rows 713+3062+3109

Verdict

Bibee's sweeper is the projected matchup advantage, while Tyler Stephenson should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.273 xwOBA (-42 pts vs league, Pitcher). GB-mix adjustment: -25 pts (raw 0.277 -> adj 0.252); pitcher's induced bb_type mix skews toward weaker contact than the batter's career mix would suggest. Count-state blend: +21 pts (bb_type-adj 0.252 -> final 0.273); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. Tanner Bibee is in a 14-day hot stretch (-49 pts xwOBA-allowed vs season). RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.

Both tables use raw current-season PA (no season-blend, no recency decay). Overall is un-platoon-filtered and matches the player's year-to-date totals on BR / Savant — the natural "how is this player going right now" read. Platoon restricts to tonight's matchup hand, so it's apples-to-apples with the rest of the report (which is also platoon-filtered). Rolling rows are shown only when n PA ≥ 25; the season row is suppressed if there's no qualifying data. The verdict's hot/cold tail is anchored on the overall view.

PitchMarginal Usage %Batter xwOBAPitcher xwOBA allowedPitcher BB-mixAdj batter xwOBAAdj Δ (pts)Projected xwOBAProjected xBAProjected xSLGProjected Whiff %
Slider34.10.2910.232GB 51% / LD 21% / FB 25% / PU 3%0.291+00.2080.1930.26439.5

Curveball15.80.3500.229GB 55% / LD 29% / FB 8% / PU 8%0.330-200.2440.2760.29632.0

Adj Δ (pts) is the bb_type mix discount applied to the batter's per-pitch xwOBA: a 100 mph 0° grounder produces a much lower xwOBA than a 100 mph 25° line drive, so when the pitcher's induced bb_type mix is more grounder-heavy than the batter's career mix on this pitch, the projection is shifted down by the equivalent per-PA amount (and vice versa for air-ball-heavy pitchers).

bat speed 70.7 mph

PitchVAA (deg)Bat attack (deg)Swings (n)Match gap (deg)Note
Slider-7.2+14.0107+6.7swing steeper than pitch — pop-up / swing-under risk

Match gap = bat attack angle + pitch VAA. |gap| ≤ 3° = on plane; positive = swing steeper than pitch (under risk); negative = swing flatter than pitch (top risk).

Edge analysis

Per-pitch matchup result vs league (additive batter + pitcher projection minus league baseline). Edge (pts) is the xwOBA projection delta in wOBA points; positive favors the hitter, negative favors the pitcher. Tables only list pitches whose net xwOBA projection actually leans in that direction; xBA / xSLG are shown for context (each colored vs its own league baseline).

50.Trea TurnerRHBPHI vs Braxton Ashcraft (RHP) PROJproj xwOBA 0.275 (-40 pts)K 18.6%BB 3.2%HR 2.4%Hit 23.1%Slight Pitcher

Trea Turner RHB vs Braxton Ashcraft RHP

window: 2026 ×1, 2025 ×0.5, 2024 ×0.25 · batter rows 702+2484+2122, pitcher rows 684+1107+0

Verdict

Ashcraft's slider is the projected matchup advantage, while Trea Turner should look to drive the 4-seam fastball. Projection: 0.275 xwOBA (-40 pts vs league, Pitcher). Count-state blend: +16 pts (bb_type-adj 0.259 -> final 0.275); pitcher's count distribution pushes the batter into more favorable counts relative to how the batter performs across counts. RHB vs RHP — same-side platoon disadvantage to the hitter.
